title: Coinbase’s new tool can help agents trade and pay for premium research
slides: Coinbase’s tool lets agents trade—but with whose rules?
It also pays for premium research in real time.
The twist: agents now sign their own crypto transactions.
You no longer need manual approvals for each trade.
Test one autonomous trade today and watch execution speed.
thread_text:
1. Who signs the trade?

Coinbase’s new tool lets AI agents sign crypto transactions themselves — no human key needed.

2. Real-time research pays.

The same system pays for premium data mid-trade, giving agents live market intel.

3. No more manual checks.

You can skip approval queues entirely. The agent executes faster than you can click confirm.

4. Rules are still missing.

While the agent acts alone, regulatory guardrails haven’t been defined. Who’s liable?

5. Test one trade today.

Run a single autonomous trade on Coinbase’s testnet this week. Did it execute faster than your manual process?
video_script: Coinbase agents now sign their own trades.
They buy premium research in real time.
Try one autonomous trade and see.

script: Coinbase agents now sign their own trades.
They buy premium research in real time.
Try one autonomous trade and see.

instagram.caption:
Coinbase’s new tool lets AI agents sign their own crypto transactions—no human key required. 🤖

It pays for premium market research automatically during a trade. That’s live intel without you clicking “confirm.”

You skip the manual approval queue entirely—execution happens at machine speed. ⚡

For anyone running automated trading or content workflows: you no longer sit between the agent and the trade.

Who writes the rules when your agent acts alone—and who’s liable if it goes wrong? 💭
